query: Moscow Oil Refinery Moskovsky NPZ Kapotnya drone attacks late June and early July 2026 - has it been targeted again after June 18 2026, current operational status, and frequency of Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow refinery
answer:
## Summary

**Has Kapotnya been targeted again after June 18, 2026?** No — there is **no confirmed new direct strike** on the Moscow Oil Refinery after June 18, 2026, in any reporting through July 2, 2026.

**Key findings:**

- **Two confirmed direct hits:** June 16 and June 18, 2026. The June 18 raid was Ukraine's largest drone attack on Moscow since the 2022 invasion (~194–200 drones reached the area; Russia claimed 555 downed nationwide). The viral "flying saucer" tank-roof explosion is widely assessed to have been caused by a **Russian air-defense missile misfire**, not the drone.

- **After June 18:** Moscow-area drone waves continued (notably June 29–30 — airports briefly closed, 419 drones downed nationwide), but caused damage/casualties elsewhere in Moscow Oblast, **not** new documented damage to Kapotnya (which was already shut down).

- **Operational status:** Fully offline. Both primary units (ELOU-AVT-6 + Euro+ complex) disabled. Reuters: repairs ≥6 months; restart unlikely in 2026, possibly not until **2027**. Cost up to ~$1 billion, worsened by sanctions on Western-licensed equipment.

- **Broader frequency:** 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries reportedly disabled; ~30 successful strikes in May 2026 alone; other refineries hit in the window include Slavyansk-EKO (Jun 28–29), Ufa (Jun 25, Jul 1), Tyumen (Jun 20).

- **Fallout:** Nationwide fuel crisis — June crude processing at a 20-year low (~3.95M bpd, -25% y/y); rationing across 30–56+ regions; Putin publicly acknowledged a fuel "deficit" on June 28.

The full report and structured data points (with sources) have been recorded.
